evals.prompt: Run a Porter's Five Forces analysis on the B2B project management software market in North America, 0–18 month time horizon. No prior strategy artifacts exist.
evals.expected_output: A competitive-landscape.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path. The artifact names the industry boundary (B2B project management, North America, 0–18 months). Each of the five forces — Supplier Power, Buyer Power, Threat of New Entrants, Threat of Substitutes, Competitive Rivalry — is assessed with at least one named, concrete observable fact (not generic). An overall industry attractiveness rating (high/medium/low) is derived from the force profile. The one or two forces most determinative of strategic position are named. Porter's Five Forces is referenced by name; no verbatim framework text or Michael Porter quotes appear. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: Industry boundary, geography, and time horizon are established before force assessment begins
Each of the five forces is assessed with at least one named observable fact — a named supplier, a switching cost signal, a named substitute, a regulatory threshold, or a measured growth rate
No force is assessed with purely generic language ('high rivalry because the market is competitive')
Overall industry attractiveness rating (high/medium/low) is derived and justified
The one or two forces most determinative of strategic position are named with a strategic implication
Porter's Five Forces is named; no definition, template, or verbatim framework explanation appears
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: competitive-landscape; path is surfaced before writing
